text.title: Database Fixtures
text.summary: Django TestCase vs pytest-django, transactional rollback, SQLAlchemy session fixtures, and xdist sequence collision prevention.

section.title: Django: pytest-django vs TestCase
section.rule: Prefer pytest-django with @pytest.mark.django_db over unittest.TestCase for new tests.
Use transaction=True only when the test needs to see committed data (e.g., Celery tasks, multi-thread scenarios).

section.title: SQLAlchemy Transactional Fixture
section.rule: Use a nested transaction with SAVEPOINT to roll back after each test without recreating the DB.

section.title: xdist: Parallel DB Isolation
section.rule: When using pytest-xdist, each worker needs its own database to avoid constraint collisions.
Use the worker_id fixture from pytest-xdist to generate per-worker DB names.

section.title: Sequence Collision Prevention

antipattern.description: Using factory_boy Sequence without xdist-aware reset causes duplicate key errors
antipattern.reason: Each worker starts its counter at 0. Use factory.Sequence(lambda n: f"{worker_id}_{n}") or UUIDs as primary keys.

section.rule: Prefer UUIDs over sequential integers for test object IDs to avoid collision in parallel runs.
